2603.16415.title: IndexRAG: Bridging Facts for Cross-Document Reasoning at Index Time
2603.16415.paper_summary: Multi-hop question answering (QA) requires reasoning across multiple documents, yet existing retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) approaches address this either through graph-based methods requiring additional online processing or iterative multi-step reasoning. We present IndexRAG, a novel approach that shifts cross-document reasoning from online inference to offline indexing. IndexRAG identifies bridge entities shared across documents and generates bridging facts as independently retrievable units, requiring no additional training or fine-tuning. Experiments on three widely-used multi-hop QA benchmarks (HotpotQA, 2WikiMultiHopQA, MuSiQue) show that IndexRAG improves F1 over Naive RAG by 4.6 points on average, while requiring only single-pass retrieval and a single LLM call at inference time. When combined with IRCoT, IndexRAG outperforms all graph-based baselines on average, including HippoRAG and FastGraphRAG, while relying solely on flat retrieval. Our code will be released upon acceptance.
2603.16415.paper_summary_zh: 多跳問題回答（QA）需要在多個文檔之間進行推理，但現有的檢索增強生成（RAG）方法要麼通過需要額外在線處理的基於圖的方法來解決這個問題，要麼通過迭代的多步推理來解決。我們提出了IndexRAG，一種新穎的方法，將跨文檔推理從在線推理轉移到離線索引。IndexRAG識別在文檔之間共享的橋接實體，並生成作為獨立可檢索單元的橋接事實，無需額外的訓練或微調。在三個廣泛使用的多跳QA基準（HotpotQA、2WikiMultiHopQA、MuSiQue）上的實驗顯示，IndexRAG的F1分數平均比Naive RAG提高了4.6分，同時在推理時僅需單次檢索和一次LLM調用。當與IRCoT結合時，IndexRAG在所有基於圖的基準上平均表現優於HippoRAG和FastGraphRAG，同時僅依賴於平面檢索。我們的代碼將在接受後發布。
2603.16415.author: Zhenghua Bao et.al.
